metadata.title: Why one always-present token secretly hijacks every attention statistic
metadata.topic: The BOS token as an attention sink that corrupts aggregate metrics
metadata.purpose: When no key is strongly preferred, softmax must still spend its probability somewhere; the ever-present, semantically-neutral BOS token becomes the low-resistance sink where heads park leftover attention — so any max-reduction that includes it reports the sink, not the structure.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro am_onyx) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: StreamingLLM/KV-cache uses of sinks, why BOS acquires this during training
metadata.visual_object: An attention heatmap with column 0 glowing across every row; mask it and diverse real patterns light up in the remaining columns.
metadata.hook: Including a single structural token in your aggregation makes every meaningful attention pattern invisible.

beats.narration_text: For head 3, layer 4, scanning 50k sequences, the max-attention position is token 0 with weight > 0.55 in 91% of cases; the real verb→subject dependency only appears once position 0 is excluded from the max.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: Softmax forces attention to sum to 1 regardless of content; why should one specific position consistently absorb the plurality of the mass across thousands of diverse inputs?
Including a single structural token in your aggregation makes every meaningful attention pattern invisible.

When no key is strongly preferred, softmax must still spend its probability somewhere; the ever-present, semantically-neutral BOS token becomes the low-resistance sink where heads park leftover attention — so any max-reduction that includes it reports the sink, not the structure.
beats.visual: An attention heatmap with column 0 glowing across every row; mask it and diverse real patterns light up in the remaining columns.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: For "sat" in ["BOS","The","cat","sat",…] weights are [0.58,0.04,0.12,0.09,…]; position 0 wins the max in 94% of 1000 sequences; after exclusion, "sat"→"cat" wins 68%.

When no key is strongly preferred, softmax must still spend its probability somewhere; the ever-present, semantically-neutral BOS token becomes the low-resistance sink where heads park leftover attention — so any max-reduction that includes it reports the sink, not the structure.
